title: Shared parts should not be created with new
description: "Shared" parts should not be created with "new"Analyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugmefpitfallWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoMarking a class withPartCreationPolicy(CreationPolicy.Shared), which is part ofManaged Extensibility Framework (MEF), means that a single, shared instance of the exported object will be created. Therefore it doesn’t make sense to create new instances using the constructor and it will most likely result in unexpected behaviours.This rule raises an issue when a constructor of a class marked shared with aPartCreationPolicyAttributeis invoked.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rig
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Shared MEF parts should not be created with 'new'. Use the MEF container instead.

logic.reasoning: MEF shared parts (classes with naming convention like SharedPart, SharedService, etc.) should not be instantiated with 'new' as they are designed for singleton pattern managed by MEF container. This is a heuristic check based on naming patterns.
